representative.title: [correctness] Trigger-scope sweep gives `idea` a spec-chain precondition that contradicts its entry-point role
representative.rationale: The sweep appends `Scope: only within an Agent SDLC run (a spec chain exists), not on the bare word alone.` to `idea`, but `idea` is the bootstrap stage — its own text says 'Use at the very start of any feature or project, BEFORE acceptance criteria or any design', i.e. before any spec chain exists. The clause is logically false for this skill and can suppress the pipeline's entry-point trigger exactly when it is needed. Tellingly, the other entry point (`getting-started`) was correctly given the non-precondition scope 'when starting or navigating an Agent SDLC run'.
representative.suggestion: Give `idea` an entry-point-appropriate scope mirroring getting-started, e.g. `Scope: when starting a feature or project under Agent SDLC`, dropping the '(a spec chain exists)' precondition that cannot hold at the idea stage.
